title: Bismillah
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ
transliteration: Bismillaahir-Rahmaanir-Raheem
translation_en: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
translation_ur: اللہ کے نام سے جو بڑا مہربان نہایت رحم والا ہے
explanation: This is the opening of 113 Surahs in the Quran. Reciting Bismillah before any action invites Allah's blessing and protection into that act. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Any important matter not begun with Bismillah is cut off from blessing.' (Abu Dawud)
benefit: Brings barakah (blessing) to every action

title: Before Eating
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَعَلَى بَرَكَةِ اللَّهِ
transliteration: Bismillaahi wa 'alaa barakatillaah
translation_en: In the name of Allah and with the blessings of Allah
translation_ur: اللہ کے نام سے اور اللہ کی برکت پر
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ instructed us to begin every meal with Bismillah. If one forgets at the start, they should say 'Bismillahi awwalahu wa aakhirahu' (In the name of Allah, at its beginning and end). This dua prevents Shaytan from participating in our food.
benefit: Protects food from Shaytan and brings barakah

title: After Eating
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا وَجَعَلَنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
transliteration: Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee at'amanaa wasaqaanaa waja'alanaa muslimeen
translation_en: All praise is for Allah who fed us, gave us drink, and made us Muslims
translation_ur: تمام تعریفیں اللہ کے لیے جس نے ہمیں کھلایا پلایا اور مسلمان بنایا
explanation: After eating, we thank Allah for three blessings simultaneously: food, water, and the greatest blessing — Islam. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever recites this after eating, his past sins are forgiven.
benefit: Sins are forgiven after each meal

title: Forgot Bismillah Before Eating
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ
transliteration: Bismillaahi awwalahu wa aakhirahu
translation_en: In the name of Allah, at its beginning and at its end
translation_ur: اللہ کے نام سے، اس کے شروع میں بھی اور اس کے آخر میں بھی
explanation: If you forget to say Bismillah before eating and remember during the meal, recite this. The Prophet ﷺ said this is enough to keep Shaytan away from the remaining food, even though he was already sharing it.
benefit: Removes Shaytan from the meal even if Bismillah was forgotten

title: Before Sleeping
arabic: بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ أَمُوتُ وَأَحْيَا
transliteration: Bismika Allaahumma amootu wa ahyaa
translation_en: In Your name O Allah, I die and I live
translation_ur: اے اللہ! تیرے نام سے مرتا ہوں اور جیتا ہوں
explanation: Sleep is referred to as the 'minor death' in Islam. This dua reminds us that life and death are both in Allah's hands. The Prophet ﷺ recited this every night before sleeping, expressing total reliance on Allah.
benefit: Protection throughout the night; connects sleep to remembrance of Allah

title: Ayatul Kursi Before Sleep
arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
transliteration: Allaahu laa ilaaha illaa huwal-hayyul-qayyoom...
translation_en: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence...
translation_ur: اللہ کے سوا کوئی معبود نہیں، وہ زندہ اور سب کا تھامنے والا ہے...
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ told Abu Hurairah that reciting Ayatul Kursi before sleep ensures that a guardian from Allah protects you all night, and Shaytan cannot come near you. It is the greatest verse of the Quran. Read the full Ayah (2:255) for complete effect.
benefit: A guardian angel protects you the entire night

title: Upon Waking Up
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
transliteration: Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee ahyaanaa ba'da maa amaatanaa wa ilayhin-nushoor
translation_en: Praise be to Allah who gave us life after He caused us to die, and to Him is the resurrection
translation_ur: تمام تعریفیں اللہ کے لیے جس نے ہمیں موت کے بعد زندگی دی اور اسی کی طرف لوٹنا ہے
explanation: Waking up is a gift — not everyone who sleeps wakes up. This dua expresses gratitude for the new day while acknowledging the ultimate resurrection. The Prophet ﷺ said this every morning without fail.
benefit: Begins the day with gratitude and awareness of the Hereafter

title: Morning Dhikr
arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ
transliteration: Asbahna wa asbahal-mulku lillaah, walhamdu lillaah, laa ilaaha illallaahu wahdahu laa shareeka lah
translation_en: We have reached morning, and dominion belongs to Allah. Praise be to Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, with no partner
translation_ur: ہم نے صبح کی اور ساری بادشاہت اللہ کی ہوئی، تمام تعریفیں اللہ کے لیے
explanation: This morning dhikr establishes Tawheed (oneness of Allah) at the start of each day. The scholars say whoever begins his day with this dhikr is under divine protection for the entire day. Read after Fajr prayer.
benefit: Divine protection for the entire day

title: Evening Dhikr
arabic: أَمْسَيْنَا وَأَمْسَى الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ
transliteration: Amsaynaa wa amsal-mulku lillaah, walhamdu lillaah, laa ilaaha illallaahu wahdahu laa shareeka lah
translation_en: We have reached evening and dominion belongs to Allah. Praise be to Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, with no partner
translation_ur: ہم نے شام کی اور ساری بادشاہت اللہ کی ہوئی
explanation: The evening counterpart of the morning dhikr. Recite after Asr prayer. It closes the day with the same declaration of Allah's sovereignty with which it was opened, creating a beautiful circle of remembrance.
benefit: Protection throughout the night

title: Morning Protection Dua
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا، وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا، وَبِكَ نَحْيَا، وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ، وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ
transliteration: Allaahumma bika asbahna, wa bika amsaynaa, wa bika nahya, wa bika namootu, wa ilaykan-nushoor
translation_en: O Allah, by You we enter morning, by You we enter evening, by You we live, by You we die, and to You is the resurrection
translation_ur: اے اللہ! تیری توفیق سے ہم نے صبح کی اور تیری توفیق سے شام کی، تیری وجہ سے جیتے ہیں اور تیری وجہ سے مریں گے
explanation: This comprehensive dua covers the entire cycle of human existence — morning, evening, life, death, and resurrection — all attributed to Allah's will. It reinforces complete dependence on Allah.
benefit: Affirms total reliance on Allah for all of life

title: Entering Toilet
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْخُبُثِ وَالْخَبَائِثِ
transliteration: Allaahumma innee a'oothu bika minal-khubuthi wal-khabaa'ith
translation_en: O Allah, I seek refuge with You from male and female evil (jinn)
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میں تیری پناہ مانگتا ہوں نر اور مادہ شیاطین سے
explanation: The toilet is considered a place where evil jinn reside. The Prophet ﷺ taught us to enter with the left foot first while reciting this dua, creating a barrier of protection. Say it before entering, not inside.
benefit: Protection from evil jinn in the bathroom

title: Leaving Toilet
arabic: غُفْرَانَكَ
transliteration: Ghufraanak
translation_en: I seek Your forgiveness
translation_ur: میں تیری معافی مانگتا ہوں
explanation: When leaving the toilet, we exit with the right foot and say this brief but powerful dua. The Prophet ﷺ was asked why we seek forgiveness after the toilet — the scholars explain it is because we were in a state where we couldn't remember Allah, and we seek forgiveness for that lapse.
benefit: Forgiveness for momentary neglect of Allah's remembrance

title: Entering Home
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَلَجْنَا، وَبِسْمِ اللَّهِ خَرَجْنَا، وَعَلَى اللَّهِ رَبِّنَا تَوَكَّلْنَا
transliteration: Bismillaahi walajna, wa bismillaahi kharajna, wa 'alallaahi rabbinaa tawakkalnaa
translation_en: In the name of Allah we enter, in the name of Allah we leave, and upon Allah our Lord we trust
translation_ur: اللہ کے نام سے داخل ہوئے، اللہ کے نام سے نکلے اور اللہ پر بھروسہ کیا
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said when a person enters his home and mentions Allah's name upon entering and eating, Shaytan tells his companions: 'You have no place to stay here and no food tonight.' This dua keeps your home protected from Shaytan.
benefit: Prevents Shaytan from entering and staying in your home

title: Leaving Home
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ، وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
transliteration: Bismillaahi, tawakkaltu 'alallaah, wa laa hawla wa laa quwwata illaa billaah
translation_en: In the name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah, and there is no might nor power except with Allah
translation_ur: اللہ کے نام سے، اللہ پر بھروسہ کیا، اور نہیں ہے طاقت نہ قوت مگر اللہ سے
explanation: When this dua is recited upon leaving home, an angel says: 'You are guided, defended, and protected.' Shaytan moves away and says to other devils, 'How can you do anything to a man who has been guided, defended, and protected?'
benefit: An angel guides and protects you throughout your journey

title: Dua for Travel
arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ، وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ
transliteration: Subhaanal-lathee sakhkhara lanaa haathaa wa maa kunnaa lahoo muqrineen, wa innaa ilaa rabbinaa lamunqaliboon
translation_en: Glory to Him who subjected this for us, and we were not able to do it ourselves. And indeed, to our Lord we will return
translation_ur: پاک ہے وہ جس نے اسے ہمارے لیے مسخر کیا، ہم اسے قابو نہیں کر سکتے تھے، اور ہمیں اپنے رب کی طرف لوٹنا ہے
explanation: This Quranic dua (43:13-14) is read when mounting any vehicle — car, airplane, ship, etc. It acknowledges that these means of transport are gifts from Allah that we couldn't create or control on our own, and reminds us of our return to Him.
benefit: Safety and protection throughout the journey

title: Returning from Travel
arabic: آيِبُونَ، تَائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنَا حَامِدُونَ
transliteration: Aayiboona, taa'iboona, 'aabidoona, lirabbinaa haamidoon
translation_en: Returning, repenting, worshipping, and praising our Lord
translation_ur: لوٹنے والے، توبہ کرنے والے، عبادت گزار، اپنے رب کی حمد کرنے والے
explanation: When coming back from a journey, the Prophet ﷺ would say this dua at the end of each high point during travel. It expresses the traveler's gratitude for safe return and commitment to worship. Say it as you near home.
benefit: Gratitude for safe return; renewal of worship commitment

title: Dua for Anxiety & Grief
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي عَبْدُكَ ابْنُ عَبْدِكَ ابْنُ أَمَتِكَ نَاصِيَتِي بِيَدِكَ مَاضٍ فِيَّ حُكْمُكَ عَدْلٌ فِيَّ قَضَاؤُكَ
transliteration: Allaahumma innee 'abduka ibnu 'abdika ibnu amatika, naasiyatee biyadika, maadin fiyya hukmuka, 'adlun fiyya qadaa'uk
translation_en: O Allah, I am Your servant, son of Your servant, son of Your female servant. My forelock is in Your hand. Your command over me is forever executed, and Your decree over me is just
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میں تیرا بندہ ہوں، تیرے بندے کا بیٹا، تیری بندی کا بیٹا، میری پیشانی تیرے ہاتھ میں ہے، مجھ میں تیرا حکم جاری ہے
explanation: This is one of the most powerful duas for depression, anxiety and grief. The Prophet ﷺ promised: 'Allah will take away his sadness and grief and replace it with happiness.' It is a full acknowledgment of one's servitude to Allah and trust in His wisdom. The full dua continues with asking for specific relief.
benefit: Allah replaces sadness with happiness for whoever recites it

title: Sayyidul Istighfar — Master of Forgiveness
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ، وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ
transliteration: Allaahumma anta rabbee laa ilaaha illaa ant, khalaqtanee wa ana 'abduka, wa ana 'alaa 'ahdika wa wa'dika mastata't
translation_en: O Allah, You are my Lord, none has the right to be worshipped except You. You created me and I am Your servant, and I abide by Your covenant and promise as best I can
translation_ur: اے اللہ! تو میرا رب ہے، تیرے سوا کوئی معبود نہیں، تو نے مجھے پیدا کیا اور میں تیرا بندہ ہوں
explanation: Called the 'Master of Seeking Forgiveness', this is the most comprehensive form of istighfar. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says it with firm belief in the morning, and dies that day before evening, will be among the people of Paradise. And whoever says it in the evening with firm belief, and dies before morning, will be among the people of Paradise.' (Bukhari)
benefit: Whoever believes and recites this morning/evening is guaranteed Paradise if they die that day

title: Seeking Forgiveness
arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
transliteration: Astaghfirul-laahal-lathee laa ilaaha illaa huwal-hayyul-qayyoomu wa atoobu ilayh
translation_en: I seek forgiveness from Allah, there is no god but He, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer, and I repent to Him
translation_ur: میں اللہ سے معافی مانگتا ہوں جس کے سوا کوئی معبود نہیں، وہ ہمیشہ زندہ اور قائم رہنے والا ہے
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever recites this dua, their sins will be forgiven even if they were like the foam of the ocean or the number of grains of sand. This is a quick, powerful dua that can be read between any acts of worship.
benefit: Sins forgiven even if as many as the foam of the ocean

title: Entering the Masjid
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ افْتَحْ لِي أَبْوَابَ رَحْمَتِكَ
transliteration: Allaahumm-aftah lee abwaaba rahmatik
translation_en: O Allah, open for me the gates of Your mercy
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میرے لیے اپنی رحمت کے دروازے کھول دے
explanation: Enter the masjid with the right foot and recite this dua. The masjid is the house of Allah, and entering it is an act of worship in itself. By asking for the gates of mercy to open, we are requesting Allah's complete rahma to descend upon us during our time in His house.
benefit: Allah's mercy descends upon you while you are in the masjid

title: Leaving the Masjid
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ
transliteration: Allaahumma innee as'aluka min fadlik
translation_en: O Allah, I ask You of Your bounty
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میں تجھ سے تیرا فضل مانگتا ہوں
explanation: Exit the masjid with the left foot and recite this. Just as we ask for mercy upon entering, we ask for Allah's bounty (rizq, sustenance, goodness in worldly affairs) upon leaving. This connects the sacred space to our daily worldly life.
benefit: Allah's bounty and provision in worldly affairs

title: Dua-e-Qunoot (Witr)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنَا فِيمَنْ هَدَيْتَ، وَعَافِنَا فِيمَنْ عَافَيْتَ، وَتَوَلَّنَا فِيمَنْ تَوَلَّيْتَ، وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِيمَا أَعْطَيْتَ
transliteration: Allaahumm-ahdinaa feeman hadayt, wa 'aafinaa feeman 'aafayt, wa tawallanaa feeman tawallayt, wa baarik lanaa feemaa a'tayt
translation_en: O Allah, guide us among those You have guided, grant us well-being among those You have given well-being, take us as allies among those You have taken as allies, and bless us in what You have given
translation_ur: اے اللہ! ہمیں ان لوگوں میں ہدایت دے جن کو تو نے ہدایت دی، ان میں عافیت دے جن کو تو نے عافیت دی
explanation: Dua-e-Qunoot is recited in the last raka'ah of Witr prayer, in the standing position after ruku' (according to one view) or before ruku' (according to another). It is a comprehensive supplication covering guidance, health, divine support, and blessing.
benefit: Comprehensive supplication covering all worldly and spiritual needs

title: After Adhan
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ هَذِهِ الدَّعْوَةِ التَّامَّةِ، وَالصَّلَاةِ الْقَائِمَةِ، آتِ مُحَمَّدًا الْوَسِيلَةَ وَالْفَضِيلَةَ
transliteration: Allaahumma rabba haathihid-da'watit-taammah, was-salaatil-qaa'imah, aati muhammadanil-waseelata wal-fadeelah
translation_en: O Allah, Lord of this perfect call and the prayer to be offered, grant Muhammad the intercession and the distinguished rank
translation_ur: اے اللہ! اس کامل پکار اور قائم ہونے والی نماز کے رب، محمد ﷺ کو وسیلہ اور فضیلت عطا فرما
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says this dua after hearing the adhan, my intercession will be permitted for him on the Day of Judgment.' (Bukhari). Recite it immediately after the muadhdhin finishes the adhan.
benefit: Guaranteed the Prophet's ﷺ intercession on the Day of Judgment

title: For the Sick
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ النَّاسِ، أَذْهِبِ الْبَأْسَ، اشْفِ أَنْتَ الشَّافِي، لَا شِفَاءَ إِلَّا شِفَاؤُكَ، شِفَاءً لَا يُغَادِرُ سَقَمًا
transliteration: Allaahumma rabban-naas, athhibil-ba's, ishfi antash-shaafi, laa shifaa'a illaa shifaa'uk, shifaa'an laa yughaadiiru saqamaa
translation_en: O Allah, Lord of mankind, remove the hardship and grant cure. You are the Curer, there is no cure except Your cure — a cure that leaves no illness
translation_ur: اے اللہ! لوگوں کے رب، تکلیف دور فرما، شفا دے، تو ہی شفا دینے والا ہے، تیری شفا کے سوا کوئی شفا نہیں — ایسی شفا جو کوئی بیماری نہ چھوڑے
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ recited this dua when visiting sick people, placing his hand on them. It acknowledges that true healing only comes from Allah. This should be recited over the sick person 3 or 7 times, preferably with right hand on their forehead.
benefit: Complete healing from illness — physical and spiritual

title: Ruqyah — Self Healing
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَرْقِيكَ، مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ يُؤْذِيكَ، مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ نَفْسٍ أَوْ عَيْنِ حَاسِدٍ، اللَّهُ يَشْفِيكَ
transliteration: Bismillaahi arqeek, min kulli shay'in yu'theek, min sharri kulli nafsin aw 'ayni haasid, Allaahu yashfeek
translation_en: In the name of Allah I perform ruqya for you, from everything that harms you, from the evil of every soul or envious eye, may Allah heal you
translation_ur: اللہ کے نام سے تم پر دم کرتا ہوں، ہر اس چیز سے جو تمہیں تکلیف دیتی ہے، ہر نفس یا حاسد کی نظر کے شر سے، اللہ تمہیں شفا دے
explanation: This is the ruqyah (healing recitation) of Jibreel (as) that he recited over the Prophet ﷺ when he was ill. It can be recited over yourself or others. Place your right hand on the area of pain and recite it 3 times.
benefit: Protection from evil eye, jealousy, and physical illness

title: When it Rains
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَيِّبًا نَافِعًا
transliteration: Allaahumma sayyiban naafi'aa
translation_en: O Allah, make it a beneficial rain
translation_ur: اے اللہ! اسے نفع بخش بارش بنا
explanation: Rain is a mercy from Allah. When rain falls, duas are more likely to be accepted. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Two duas are never rejected — the dua at the time of the adhan, and the dua at the time of rain.' Ask for all your needs during rainfall.
benefit: Duas are most accepted during rainfall

title: Looking in the Mirror
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ حَسَّنْتَ خَلْقِي فَحَسِّنْ خُلُقِي
transliteration: Allaahumma anta hassanta khalqee fahassin khuluqee
translation_en: O Allah, just as You have made my physical appearance good, make my character good as well
translation_ur: اے اللہ! جیسے تو نے میری ظاہری شکل و صورت اچھی بنائی، ویسے ہی میرے اخلاق بھی اچھے بنا دے
explanation: This beautiful dua reminds us that external beauty is from Allah, and we should ask Him to match it with internal beauty of character. The Prophet ﷺ would recite this when looking at his reflection, teaching us to value akhlaq (character) above all.
benefit: Good character (akhlaq) is more valuable than good looks

title: Dua for Knowledge
arabic: رَّبِّ زِدۡنِي عِلۡمٗا
transliteration: Rabbi zidnee 'ilmaa
translation_en: My Lord, increase me in knowledge
translation_ur: اے میرے رب! میرا علم بڑھا
explanation: This is the only place in the Quran where Allah instructs us to ask for increase in something — and that something is knowledge (ilm). This shows the supreme importance of knowledge in Islam. Recite before studying, before class, and before any learning.
benefit: Allah directly commanded us to ask for more knowledge — showing its supreme value

title: Before Studying
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ انْفَعْنِي بِمَا عَلَّمْتَنِي، وَعَلِّمْنِي مَا يَنْفَعُنِي، وَزِدْنِي عِلْمًا
transliteration: Allaahumman-fa'nee bimaa 'allamtanee, wa 'allimnee maa yanfa'unee, wa zidnee 'ilmaa
translation_en: O Allah, benefit me with what You have taught me, teach me what will benefit me, and increase me in knowledge
translation_ur: اے اللہ! مجھے اس سے فائدہ دے جو تو نے مجھے سکھایا، اور مجھے وہ سکھا جو مجھے فائدہ دے، اور میرا علم بڑھا
explanation: Knowledge without benefit is useless. This dua asks for three things: benefit from existing knowledge, new beneficial knowledge, and increase in knowledge. The Prophet ﷺ would seek refuge from knowledge that does not benefit.
benefit: Ensures knowledge becomes beneficial and not just memorization

title: Dua for Ease
arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي
transliteration: Rabbish-rah lee sadree, wa yassir lee amree
translation_en: My Lord, expand my chest and ease my task for me
translation_ur: اے میرے رب! میرا سینہ کھول دے اور میرا کام آسان کر دے
explanation: This is the dua of Prophet Musa (as) when Allah gave him the huge task of confronting Pharaoh (Quran 20:25-26). If Musa needed Allah's help for such a task, we too should ask for ease in our challenges. Recite when facing any difficulty.
benefit: Removes tightness from the heart and makes difficult tasks easy

title: Dua for Parents
arabic: رَّبِّ ارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
transliteration: Rabbir-hamhumaa kamaa rabbayaanee sagheeraa
translation_en: My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up when I was small
translation_ur: اے میرے رب! ان دونوں پر رحم فرما جیسا انہوں نے مجھے بچپن میں پالا
explanation: This Quranic dua (17:24) is one of the most important duas a child can make for their parents. It compares the mercy we ask for them to the mercy they showed us in childhood. Even if your parents have passed away, this dua benefits their souls.
benefit: Immense reward for making dua for parents; benefits their souls even after death

title: After Sneezing
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
transliteration: Alhamdulillaah
translation_en: All praise is for Allah
translation_ur: تمام تعریفیں اللہ کے لیے ہیں
explanation: Sneezing is a blessing from Allah that clears the airway. The Prophet ﷺ said we should say Alhamdulillah after sneezing. Then those who hear it say 'Yarhamukallah' (may Allah have mercy on you), and the sneezer responds 'Yahdikumullahu wa yuslihu baalakum' (may Allah guide you).
benefit: A simple social Sunnah that creates community bonding through remembrance of Allah

title: For Dua to be Accepted
arabic: يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيثُ
transliteration: Yaa Hayyu Yaa Qayyoomu birahmatika astaghees
translation_en: O Ever-Living, O Self-Sustaining Sustainer! In Your mercy do I seek relief
translation_ur: اے ہمیشہ زندہ! اے قائم رہنے والے! میں تیری رحمت سے فریاد کرتا ہوں
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said Fatima bint Asad (ra) was taught this dua by Jibreel. Recite this when you feel your duas are not being answered. Calling upon Allah by His names Al-Hayy (The Ever-Living) and Al-Qayyum (The Self-Sustaining) opens the doors of divine response.
benefit: Opens the doors of Allah's response when duas feel unanswered

title: Laylatul Qadr
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي
transliteration: Allaahumma innaka 'afuwwun tuhibbul-'afwa fa'fu 'annee
translation_en: O Allah, You are Pardoning and You love pardon, so pardon me
translation_ur: اے اللہ! تو معاف کرنے والا ہے، معافی کو پسند کرتا ہے، پس مجھے معاف فرما دے
explanation: Aisha (ra) asked the Prophet ﷺ: 'If I know which night is Laylatul Qadr, what should I say?' He replied: 'Say this.' Laylatul Qadr is better than 1000 months. If even one dua on this night is accepted, a lifetime of worship is rewarded. Keep repeating this all night.
benefit: Specifically recommended for Laylatul Qadr — better than 1000 months of worship

title: Protection from Evil Eye
arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّةِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطَانٍ وَهَامَّةٍ، وَمِنْ كُلِّ عَيْنٍ لَامَّةٍ
transliteration: A'oothu bikalimaatillaahit-taammati min kulli shaytaanin wa haammatin wa min kulli 'aynin laammah
translation_en: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from every devil, harmful thing, and from every evil eye
translation_ur: میں اللہ کے کامل کلمات کی پناہ لیتا ہوں ہر شیطان، موذی جانور اور لگنے والی نظر سے
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ used to recite this over Hasan and Husain (ra) as a protection. Evil eye (nazar) is real — the Prophet ﷺ confirmed this. Recite this for your children, family, or yourself when fear of evil eye is present. Also recite the three Quls for complete protection.
benefit: Complete protection from evil eye, jinn, and all harmful things

title: Dua for Righteous Family
arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبۡ لَنَا مِنۡ أَزۡوَٰجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّـٰتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعۡيُنٍ وَٱجۡعَلۡنَا لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
transliteration: Rabbanaa hab lanaa min azwaajinaa wa thurriyyaatinaa qurrata a'yunin waj'alnaa lil-muttaqeena imaamaa
translation_en: Our Lord, grant us from our spouses and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us leaders of the righteous
translation_ur: اے ہمارے رب! ہمیں ہماری بیویوں اور اولاد سے آنکھوں کی ٹھنڈک عطا فرما اور ہمیں متقیوں کا امام بنا
explanation: This is the dua of the servants of the Most Merciful mentioned in Surah Al-Furqan. 'Qurrata a'yun' literally means 'comfort of the eyes' — the Arabic expression for the greatest joy. Scholars say this dua asks for a family that will be a source of joy both in this world and the next.
benefit: Righteous spouse, righteous children, and leadership of the pious

title: Dua for Jannah
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْجَنَّةَ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ
transliteration: Allaahumma innee as'alukal-jannata wa a'oothu bika minan-naar
translation_en: O Allah, I ask You for Paradise and I seek refuge in You from the Fire
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میں تجھ سے جنت مانگتا ہوں اور دوزخ سے تیری پناہ مانگتا ہوں
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever asks Allah for Paradise three times, Paradise says: O Allah, enter him into Paradise. And whoever seeks refuge from the Fire three times, the Fire says: O Allah, protect him from the Fire.' (Tirmidhi). Recite this 3 times.
benefit: Paradise itself intercedes for you when you ask 3 times

title: Dua of Gratitude
arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي بِنِعْمَتِهِ تَتِمُّ الصَّالِحَاتُ
transliteration: Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee bini'matihee tatimmus-saalihaat
translation_en: Praise be to Allah by Whose blessing righteous deeds are completed
translation_ur: تمام تعریفیں اللہ کے لیے جس کی نعمتوں سے نیک کام مکمل ہوتے ہیں
explanation: Recite this when something good happens. It reminds us that all good outcomes are from Allah's blessing (ni'mah), not from our own effort or intelligence. This prevents pride and arrogance, and keeps the heart grateful.
benefit: Prevents arrogance after success; attracts more blessings

title: Seeing New Crescent Moon
arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُمَّ أَهِلَّهُ عَلَيْنَا بِالْأَمْنِ وَالْإِيمَانِ، وَالسَّلَامَةِ وَالْإِسْلَامِ، رَبِّي وَرَبُّكَ اللَّهُ
transliteration: Allaahu Akbar, Allaahumma ahillahu 'alaynaa bil-amni wal-eemaan, was-salaamati wal-islaam, rabbee wa rabbukallah
translation_en: Allah is Greatest. O Allah, bring this crescent upon us with security, faith, safety and Islam. My Lord and your Lord is Allah
translation_ur: اللہ سب سے بڑا ہے۔ اے اللہ! اس چاند کو ہم پر امن، ایمان، سلامتی اور اسلام کے ساتھ طلوع فرما
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ would look at the new moon and say this dua. It begins a new Islamic month with the declaration of Allah's greatness and a prayer for safety, faith, and peace throughout the month. A beautiful Sunnah to revive.
benefit: Begins a new Islamic month with faith and divine protection

title: Entering Marketplace
arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَهُوَ حَيٌّ لَا يَمُوتُ
transliteration: Laa ilaaha illallaahu wahdahu laa shareeka lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamd, yuhyee wa yumeetu wa huwa hayyun laa yamoot
translation_en: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise. He gives life and causes death, and He is Ever-Living and will never die
translation_ur: اللہ کے سوا کوئی معبود نہیں، وہ اکیلا ہے، اس کا کوئی شریک نہیں، اسی کی بادشاہت اور اسی کی تعریف، وہ زندگی دیتا اور موت دیتا ہے
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever enters a marketplace and says this, Allah will write for him a million good deeds, erase from him a million sins, and raise him a million degrees.' (Tirmidhi). The marketplace is a place of heedlessness — remembering Allah there has immense reward.
benefit: A million good deeds, a million sins erased, a million ranks raised

title: When Angry
arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
transliteration: A'oothu billaahi minash-shaytaanir-rajeem
translation_en: I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Shaytan
translation_ur: میں شیطان مردود سے اللہ کی پناہ مانگتا ہوں
explanation: When a man became very angry in front of the Prophet ﷺ, he said: 'I know a word, if he said it his anger would go away — A'oothu billahi minash-shaytanir rajeem.' Anger is from Shaytan; seeking refuge from him immediately weakens the anger.
benefit: Removes anger which is from Shaytan; prevents sins committed in anger

title: Dua to Repay Debt
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِي بِحَلَالِكَ عَنْ حَرَامِكَ، وَأَغْنِنِي بِفَضْلِكَ عَمَّنْ سِوَاكَ
transliteration: Allaahumak-finee bihalaalika 'an haraamika, wa aghninee bifadlika 'amman siwaak
translation_en: O Allah, suffice me with what You have made lawful, so that I have no need for what You have made unlawful, and make me independent through Your bounty from all others besides You
translation_ur: اے اللہ! مجھے حلال سے اتنا عطا فرما کہ حرام کی ضرورت نہ رہے، اور اپنے فضل سے مجھے تیرے سوا سب سے بے نیاز کر دے
explanation: A man who was burdened with debt said this dua to Ali (ra), who said the Prophet ﷺ taught it to him. He said: 'If you have debt like a mountain, Allah will pay it for you.' It asks for halal sufficiency instead of resorting to haram to cover debts.
benefit: Allah removes debt even if it is as large as a mountain

title: Dua for Rizq (Sustenance)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ رِزْقًا طَيِّبًا، وَعِلْمًا نَافِعًا، وَعَمَلًا مُتَقَبَّلًا
transliteration: Allaahumma innee as'aluka rizqan tayyibaa, wa 'ilman naafi'aa, wa 'amalan mutaqabbalaa
translation_en: O Allah, I ask You for good sustenance, beneficial knowledge, and accepted deeds
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میں تجھ سے پاکیزہ رزق، نفع بخش علم اور مقبول عمل مانگتا ہوں
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ would say this dua after Fajr prayer. It combines three of the greatest requests: halal and pure sustenance, knowledge that benefits in this life and the next, and deeds that are accepted by Allah. The scholars say whoever gets these three has achieved complete success.
benefit: Three complete blessings: pure rizq, beneficial knowledge, accepted deeds

title: Dua for Sincerity
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أُشْرِكَ بِكَ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ، وَأَسْتَغْفِرُكَ لِمَا لَا أَعْلَمُ
transliteration: Allaahumma innee a'oothu bika an ushrika bika wa ana a'lam, wa astaghfiruka limaa laa a'lam
translation_en: O Allah, I seek refuge with You from knowingly associating partners with You, and I seek Your forgiveness for what I do not know
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میں تیری پناہ مانگتا ہوں کہ جانتے ہوئے تیرے ساتھ شرک کروں، اور جو میں نہیں جانتا اس کی معافی مانگتا ہوں
explanation: Hidden shirk (riya — showing off) is one of the most dangerous sins. This dua seeks protection from all forms of shirk, including the subtle ones we may not be aware of. Recite it frequently to purify your intentions.
benefit: Protection from hidden shirk and riya (showing off)

title: Dua-e-Istikhara
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْتَخِيرُكَ بِعِلْمِكَ، وَأَسْتَقْدِرُكَ بِقُدْرَتِكَ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْعَظِيمِ
transliteration: Allaahumma innee astakheeruka bi'ilmika, wa astaqdiruka biqudratika, wa as'aluka min fadlikal-'azeem
translation_en: O Allah, I seek Your counsel through Your knowledge, I seek Your assistance through Your power, and I ask of You Your great bounty
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میں تیرے علم سے بھلائی مانگتا ہوں، تیری قدرت سے مدد مانگتا ہوں اور تیرے عظیم فضل کا سوالی ہوں
explanation: Salat-ul-Istikhara: Pray 2 rakaat of nafl salah, then recite this full dua (inserting the matter you're deciding at the appropriate place). The Prophet ﷺ said he taught the companions Istikhara for all matters as he taught them Quranic surahs. The result comes through feelings, circumstances, or ease of path — not necessarily a dream.
benefit: Allah guides your decision — His knowledge surpasses all human judgment

title: Dua for Good Health
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَدَنِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي سَمْعِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَصَرِي
transliteration: Allaahumma 'aafinee fee badanee, Allaahumma 'aafinee fee sam'ee, Allaahumma 'aafinee fee basaree
translation_en: O Allah, grant me health in my body. O Allah, grant me health in my hearing. O Allah, grant me health in my sight
translation_ur: اے اللہ! مجھے میرے جسم میں عافیت دے۔ اے اللہ! مجھے میری سماعت میں عافیت دے۔ اے اللہ! مجھے میری بصارت میں عافیت دے
explanation: Abu Bakr (ra) asked the Prophet ﷺ to teach him a dua to recite morning and evening. This was among the duas he was taught. It covers complete health — body, hearing, and sight — the three most critical physical faculties. Recite morning and evening.
benefit: Complete physical health — body, hearing, and sight

title: Subhanallah — Glory to Allah
arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
transliteration: Subhaanal-laahi wa bihamdih
translation_en: Glory be to Allah and praise Him
translation_ur: اللہ پاک ہے اور اسی کی تعریف ہے
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever says Subhanallahi wa bihamdih 100 times a day will have his sins forgiven even if they are as numerous as the foam of the sea.' (Bukhari). He also said it is the heaviest on the Scale on the Day of Judgment. Say it 100 times — takes only 5 minutes.
benefit: 100 times: sins forgiven even if like foam of the sea; heaviest dhikr on the Scales

title: Kalima Tayyiba
arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ
transliteration: Laa ilaaha illallaahu Muhammadur-rasoolullaah
translation_en: There is no god worthy of worship except Allah, and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah
translation_ur: اللہ کے سوا کوئی معبود نہیں اور محمد ﷺ اللہ کے رسول ہیں
explanation: This is the first Kalima of Islam — the key to Paradise. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever dies knowing that there is no god worthy of worship but Allah, will enter Paradise.' (Muslim). It is the most important sentence a Muslim can say. Renew it constantly.
benefit: The key to Paradise — whoever dies upon this enters Jannah

title: Morning Protection (3x)
arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
transliteration: Bismillaahil-lathee laa yadurru ma'asmihi shay'un fil-ardi wa laa fis-samaa'i wa huwas-samee'ul-'aleem
translation_en: In the name of Allah with whose name nothing can harm — neither on earth nor in the heavens — and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing
translation_ur: اللہ کے نام سے جس کے نام کے ساتھ زمین و آسمان میں کوئی چیز نقصان نہیں دے سکتی، اور وہ سننے والا جاننے والا ہے
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever recites this 3 times in the morning and 3 times in the evening, nothing will harm him.' (Abu Dawud, Tirmidhi). This is one of the most powerful morning/evening adhkar. Recite 3 times after Fajr and 3 times after Asr.
benefit: Recited 3x morning and 3x evening: nothing can harm you that day/night

title: After Wudu
arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَّا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُهُ وَرَسُولُهُ
transliteration: Ash-hadu allaa ilaaha illallaahu wahdahu laa shareeka lah, wa ash-hadu anna muhammadan 'abduhu wa rasooluh
translation_en: I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger
translation_ur: میں گواہی دیتا ہوں کہ اللہ کے سوا کوئی معبود نہیں، وہ اکیلا ہے، اس کا کوئی شریک نہیں، اور میں گواہی دیتا ہوں کہ محمد ﷺ اس کے بندے اور رسول ہیں
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever makes wudu and says this, the eight gates of Paradise will be opened for him, and he may enter through whichever he pleases.' (Muslim). Adding 'Allahummaj'alnee minat-tawwaabeena waj'alnee minal-mutatahhireen' is also recommended.
benefit: All 8 gates of Paradise are opened for the one who says this after wudu

title: Salah on the Prophet ﷺ (Friday)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
transliteration: Allaahumma salli 'alaa Muhammadin wa 'alaa aali Muhammad
translation_en: O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad
translation_ur: اے اللہ! محمد ﷺ اور آل محمد پر درود بھیج
explanation: On Fridays, the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Send abundant salawat on me on Friday.' For every salawat we send, Allah sends 10 blessings upon us. On Jumu'ah especially, there is a special time when duas are accepted — some scholars say it is the last hour before Asr.
benefit: 10 blessings from Allah for every salawat; special reward on Fridays

title: Tasbeeh Fatima (Before Sleep)
arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ (٣٣)  الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (٣٣)  اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ (٣٤)
transliteration: Subhaanallaah (33x), Alhamdulillaah (33x), Allaahu Akbar (34x)
translation_en: Glory be to Allah (33x), All praise is for Allah (33x), Allah is the Greatest (34x)
translation_ur: اللہ پاک ہے (33 بار)، تمام تعریفیں اللہ کے لیے (33 بار)، اللہ سب سے بڑا ہے (34 بار)
explanation: When Fatima (ra) asked the Prophet ﷺ for a servant to help with housework, he taught her this instead: 'It is better for you than a servant.' Reciting this 100 dhikr before sleeping gives strength for the next day, makes hardship easy, and brings barakah. Total: 100 dhikr.
benefit: Better than worldly wealth; gives strength, removes tiredness; replaces need for a servant

title: Dua Before Exam/Test
arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي، وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي، وَاحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِي، يَفْقَهُوا قَوْلِي
transliteration: Rabbish-rah lee sadree, wa yassir lee amree, wahlul 'uqdatam-min lisaanee, yafqahoo qawlee
translation_en: My Lord, expand my chest, ease my task for me, and untie the knot in my tongue so they may understand my speech
translation_ur: اے میرے رب! میرا سینہ کھول دے، میرا کام آسان کر دے، اور میری زبان کی گرہ کھول دے تاکہ لوگ میری بات سمجھ سکیں
explanation: This is the dua of Prophet Musa (as) before speaking to Pharaoh — the biggest test of his life. Recite it before any exam, presentation, interview, or public speaking. The dua specifically asks for: mental clarity, ease of the task, and ability to express yourself clearly.
benefit: Mental clarity, ease in the task, and clear expression — perfect for exams

title: Dua for Righteous Children
arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
transliteration: Rabbi hab lee minas-saaliheen
translation_en: My Lord, grant me offspring from among the righteous
translation_ur: اے میرے رب! مجھے نیک اولاد عطا فرما
explanation: This is the dua of Ibrahim (as) (Quran 37:100) when he asked Allah for children. Ibrahim (as) was given Ismail (as) after this prayer. Recite this frequently before marriage, during pregnancy, and throughout parenthood to ask for righteous offspring.
benefit: A righteous child is the best gift — one who prays for you after you die

title: When Hearing Thunder
arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي يُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ
transliteration: Subhaanal-lathee yusabbihur-ra'du bihamdih
translation_en: Glory be to Him whom thunder glorifies with His praise
translation_ur: پاک ہے وہ جس کی تعریف میں بادل کی گرج تسبیح کرتی ہے
explanation: The Quran tells us thunder glorifies Allah (13:13). When we hear thunder, reciting this dua aligns us with the natural world in glorifying Allah. The Prophet ﷺ would also say 'Allahu Akbar' upon seeing lightning, as it is a reminder of the power of Allah.
benefit: Joins your praise with the thunder's own glorification of Allah

title: Breaking Fast (Iftar)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ صُمْتُ، وَعَلَى رِزْقِكَ أَفْطَرْتُ
transliteration: Allaahumma laka sumtu, wa 'alaa rizqika aftart
translation_en: O Allah, for You I have fasted and upon Your provision I break my fast
translation_ur: اے اللہ! میں نے تیرے لیے روزہ رکھا اور تیرے رزق سے افطار کیا
explanation: Recited at the moment of breaking the fast. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The fasting person has two moments of joy: when he breaks his fast, and when he meets his Lord.' (Bukhari). At iftar, duas are among the most accepted. Make all your requests to Allah during this time.
benefit: Iftar time is among the most powerful times for dua acceptance

title: Intention (Niyyah) for Fasting
arabic: وَبِصَوْمِ غَدٍ نَوَيْتُ مِنْ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ
transliteration: Wa bisawmi ghadin nawaytu min shahri ramadaan
translation_en: I intend to fast tomorrow for the month of Ramadan
translation_ur: میں نے رمضان کے مہینے میں کل کے روزے کی نیت کی
explanation: The niyyah (intention) for Ramadan fasting must be made the night before (before Fajr). The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever does not intend to fast before Fajr, there is no fast for him.' (Abu Dawud). The niyyah is in the heart, but expressing it verbally is recommended.
benefit: Valid fasting requires sincere intention — the foundation of all worship

title: Salawat Ibrahimiyya
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ، كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، إِنَّكَ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ
transliteration: Allaahumma salli 'alaa Muhammadin wa 'alaa aali Muhammad, kamaa sallayta 'alaa Ibraaheema wa 'alaa aali Ibraaheem, innaka Hameedun Majeed
translation_en: O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, just as You sent blessings upon Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim. Indeed, You are Praiseworthy and Glorious
translation_ur: اے اللہ! محمد ﷺ اور آل محمد پر رحمت نازل فرما جیسا تو نے ابراہیم اور آل ابراہیم پر نازل فرمائی، بے شک تو تعریف والا اور بزرگی والا ہے
explanation: This is the Durood recited in every salah (Tashahhud). The Prophet ﷺ said sending salawat on him is an act that erases sins and raises ranks. One salawat brings 10 blessings, 10 sins erased, and 10 ranks raised. Recite abundantly — especially on Fridays.
benefit: 10 blessings, 10 sins erased, 10 ranks raised per salawat

title: Dua at the Time of Marriage
arabic: بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ، وَبَارَكَ عَلَيْكَ، وَجَمَعَ بَيْنَكُمَا فِي خَيْرٍ
transliteration: Baarakallahu laka, wa baaraka 'alayk, wa jama'a baynakumaa fee khayr
translation_en: May Allah bless you, and shower His blessings upon you, and join you together in goodness
translation_ur: اللہ آپ کو برکت دے، آپ پر برکتیں نازل فرمائے، اور آپ دونوں کو بھلائی میں جمع رکھے
explanation: This is the dua the Prophet ﷺ used to make for newlyweds. It is Sunnah to recite this upon congratulating a couple. The couple themselves should also make dua to Allah after nikah. A marriage blessed by Allah from the start is more likely to be filled with love (mawaddah) and mercy (rahma).
benefit: Blessings of Allah upon the new couple and their life together

title: Dua for Tahajjud (Night Prayer)
arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ نُورُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ
transliteration: Allaahumma lakal-hamdu anta noorun-samaawaati wal-ardi wa man feehinn
translation_en: O Allah, all praise is Yours. You are the Light of the heavens and the earth and all that is within them
translation_ur: اے اللہ! تمام تعریفیں تیرے لیے ہیں، تو آسمانوں اور زمین اور ان میں موجود سب کا نور ہے
explanation: The Prophet ﷺ used to begin Tahajjud with this dua. Tahajjud is the best voluntary prayer, and the time of its performance (last third of night) is the time when Allah descends to the lowest heaven and says: 'Who calls upon Me that I may answer? Who asks of Me that I may give?' (Bukhari)
benefit: Allah is closest to His servants in the last third of the night

title: Complete Trust in Allah
arabic: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
transliteration: Hasbunallaahu wa ni'mal-wakeel
translation_en: Sufficient for us is Allah, and He is the best Disposer of affairs
translation_ur: ہمیں اللہ کافی ہے اور وہ بہترین کارساز ہے
explanation: When Ibrahim (as) was thrown into fire, he said this. When the companions were told armies were gathering against them, they said this. Allah extinguished the fire for Ibrahim. Allah changed fear into faith for the companions. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'This is what Ibrahim (as) said when thrown into fire.'
benefit: The words of Ibrahim (as) in the fire — Allah handles all affairs for whoever says this with belief
